resources.string: Configure name, avatar, model, and parameters
Enable local tools like JavaScript engine
Choose local skills from the Termux workdir
Configure MCP server tools for the assistant
Manage assistant memory and recent chat references
Set system prompts and regex rules
Configure custom request headers and body parameters
Schedule prompts to run automatically in background
Adjust Gaussian blur strength for the chat background. Effective on Android 12 and above.
Set background image for assistant chat page
Set the default chat model for the assistant. If not set, the global default chat model will be used
Controls how many historical messages will be sent to the model as context. Messages exceeding this number will be ignored, only the most recent N messages will be retained, which can save tokens
Are you sure you want to delete this assistant? This action cannot be undone.
Execute JavaScript code with QuickJS engine for calculations and scripting
Get current date, weekday, time, and timezone info from the device
Read or write plain text from the device clipboard
Execute commands in local Termux (com.termux) via the termux_exec tool
Run Python code in Termux via the termux_python tool (requires Termux python)
When enabled, Termux tools require manual approval before running
Maximum number of tokens to generate. Leave empty for no limit.

When memory is enabled, the model will try to actively record your information during conversations and use it in subsequent conversations. This feature requires the model to support tool calls to work properly
When enabled, the assistant will reference recent chat history to provide more contextual responses
Define regex patterns to transform messages before sending/receiving
Visual Only (affects display only, not actual message)
Prompt Only (affects LLM input only, not display)
Min depth applies to the Nth message from the end and older. Max depth applies to the latest N messages.
Whether to enable streaming output for messages
Leave blank to follow thinking budget. Chat Completions: %1$s, Responses: %2$s
Effective reasoning effort sent for each OpenAI API format.
No scheduled tasks yet. Tap + to create one.
Please don\'t modify this value unless you know what you\'re doing
Use assistant avatar and name instead of model avatar and name in chats
Export app data to a file
The app needs to restart for the changes to take effect.
Please chat first before editing the title
Export conversation as a markdown file
Temporary chat enabled. This conversation will not appear in history.
Temporary chat disabled. This conversation will appear in history.
Current message count: %1$d. This may cause the application to crash or lag, or even lose this conversation data. Please create a new conversation as soon as possible to continue chatting.
This conversation data is too large
"The above content is generated by artificial intelligence and may contain inaccuracies. "
Are you sure you want to delete all conversations? This action cannot be undone.
Select aspect ratio for generated images
Are you sure you want to cancel image generation?
Number of images to generate each time
Select AI model for image generation
Describe the image you want to generate...
No available AI providers, please add in settings
No text content available to copy
Camera permission is required to take photos
Some permissions have been permanently denied, please go to the app settings to manually enable them:
In order to provide a better user experience, the app requires the following permissions:
Let the model automatically determine reasoning level.
The model will use a large amount of reasoning to answer questions.
The model will use a small amount of reasoning to answer questions.
The model will use more reasoning to answer questions.
Turn off reasoning functionality, the model will directly answer questions.
Use pure black background in dark theme, suitable for AMOLED screens
Automatically collapse thinking content after thinking is complete
Whether to display model icons in chat list messages
Automatically collapse code blocks by default
Automatically wrap long lines in code blocks
Render html/svg/xml-with-svg code blocks directly instead of showing raw code
Only Rich Render Recent Code Blocks
Only apply HTML/SVG rich rendering to code blocks within the most recent N messages, inclusive. Older messages still use normal syntax-highlighted code blocks. 0 means no limit.
Create a new conversation when the app starts
Show more debugging features. Not recommended for general users.
Enable haptic feedback when messages are being generated

At four in the morning, I saw the begonia flowers still awake. I am often like this — lying awake alone in the quiet night, thinking: if someone is thinking of me at this moment, that would be wonderful.
Move Message Jumper to left side
Show a notification when a message is generated if the app is not in the foreground
Display quick jump buttons on the right when scrolling the chat list
Whether to display model names in chat messages
Whether to show message date below nickname or model name
Display token usage and message count
Whether to display app update notifications
Whether to display user avatar in chat messages
Skip crop interface after importing images or taking photos
Add custom HTTP headers for MCP server requests
Whether to enable this MCP server
Display name for the MCP server
URL address for SSE transport server
URL address for Streamable HTTP server
Executable name or path to launch inside Termux
One argument per line. This maps to the standard args array.
Directory used when launching the local MCP server in Termux
Optional environment variables passed to the local MCP server process
Cannot view tool list when adding MCP Server, please check back after adding
Select the transport protocol type for the MCP server
Model used for optical character recognition on images
Model used to generate conversation suggestions, recommend using fast and inexpensive models
Model used to summarize conversation titles, recommend using fast and inexpensive models
The model used for translation features

Select built-in tools supported by the model. These tools are directly provided by the API without requiring app support. Support depends on the provider and model, such as Gemini built-in search/URL context and OpenAI Responses built-in search/image generation.
Enable the built-in image generation tool for supported OpenAI Responses models
You haven\'t configured API and model yet, please configure first
Set default models for each feature
View app usage instructions and help documentation
If you like it, sponsor the author\'s work
RikkaHub-Lune - Open Source Android AI Assistant\n\nWebsite: https://example.com
Click the button below to add models
Are you sure you want to delete this provider? This action cannot be undone.
Failed to parse QR code from image: %s
Configure custom provider settings for this specific model. Will inherit common properties from parent provider.
Response API is OpenAI\'s next-generation API. Most providers do not support it. If you don\'t know what this is, don\'t enable it.
Shared providers do not include model lists.
This selection is too large to fit in a QR code. Use system share instead.
Custom name for this TTS provider
Control the pitch of speech (0.1 - 2.0, 1.0 is normal)
Control the speed of speech (0.1 - 3.0, 1.0 is normal)
Hello, this is a text-to-speech test.
Voice to use for speech synthesis
* Built-in Silicon Flow free models are available without an API Key. For more powerful models, please apply and configure your own API Key.
**Leading global AI capability provider, accelerating AGI to benefit humanity.**
Tag with this name already exists
The current model does not have tool calling enabled. Please enable the model\'s tool capabilities in the settings to use features like search/MCP.
Translation result will be shown here
Click the button below to add
Default SillyTavern-style scan behavior used by all lorebooks in this app.
Prefix scanned chat lines with user and character names.
Allow triggered entries to activate more entries in later passes.
Create mode injections or lorebooks in Settings
Display line numbers in code blocks
Whether to show bubble background for assistant messages
When enabled, TTS will only read content within quotation marks
Automatically play TTS after assistant message generation completes
Are you sure you want to regenerate this message?
Compress the conversation into a summary to save tokens
Specify what to preserve or focus on
⚠️ Compressing context will reset all messages in the current conversation
When pasting text exceeds the threshold, automatically save it as a file attachment
Scroll pages with volume keys, ideal for e-ink screens

Official free AI service, using user sponsorship funds to provide some free models for new users as fallback, no API key required. Has strict rate limits, if you have purchased your own provider, please use your own provider as much as possible.
Not enough messages to compress. Reduce the number of messages to keep or add more messages.
Press Enter to send message instead of adding a new line
Use global shared memory instead of assistant-isolated memory. When enabled, all assistants share the same memory storage
Show real-time notification updates during message generation (Android 15+)
Notify when scheduled prompts complete or fail
Automatically scroll to the bottom when AI is generating content
Use the animated app icon instead of the default circular loading indicator
Allow you access RikkaHub-Lune via Web
Require password verification when accessing the web interface
Password required for web interface access
Listen only on localhost (127.0.0.1), disable LAN and mDNS access
Default working directory for termux_exec and termux_python (e.g. /data/data/com.termux/files/home/workspace)
Serve Termux workdir on localhost for Markdown images (e.g. ![](http://127.0.0.1:9090/test.png)). Requires Python in Termux.
Execute Termux tools in background without bringing Termux to the foreground (recommended)
Commands matching these rules always require approval, even when Require Approval is off. Use one rule per line or separate with commas.
Grant com.termux.permission.RUN_COMMAND to this app so Termux tools can execute commands directly.
Required to call com.termux.app.RunCommandService from this app.
Grant this app permission to read all files on your device (required for Termux tools).
Timeout for termux_exec and termux_python. Minimum 1000ms. Default 120000ms (2 minutes).
Applies to termux_exec with tty=true and write_stdin. PTY uses the Python standard library, so no pip package is required.
2) Open Termux and run this command:
4) For termux_python or interactive tty=true sessions: install Python in Termux (pkg install python). PTY does not require any extra pip package.
Render mathematical expressions and formulas using LaTeX syntax
This will clear the existing index and re-scan all chat history. The more messages, the longer it takes. Continue?
Enter keywords and press Search to find messages
Automatically inject time reminders before messages with large time gaps to help AI understand conversation intervals

LAN Address: Uses device WiFi IP, accessible to other devices on the same network. Unavailable on mobile data.\nLocal Address: Uses localhost, accessible on this device only.\nmDNS Address: Uses .local hostname, accessible by name from other devices on the same network without knowing the IP.
You have launched the app over 100 times! If you enjoy using it, please consider supporting the developer.
Let the AI speak text aloud to you using the device TTS engine
Whether to expand and show the thinking process by default during generation
Manage background scheduled tasks and execution options
Use an ongoing foreground notification to keep scheduled tasks running.
Required for precise scheduled task triggering.
This will permanently delete all finished task run records.
This record will be permanently deleted.
Paste MCP Server JSON config, supports standard mcpServers format
No valid MCP Server configuration found
Enable blur effect on chat input bar
Ask a question about this text…
Select text in any app and tap “Ask RikkaHub-Lune” from the text toolbar.
This is sample text. Long press and select it to test the selection toolbar integration.
Specify a model for this action. If not set, uses the assistant or default chat model.
Allow the AI to ask you questions for clarification
Downloading, please check status bar for progress
Tap Refresh Sessions to query Termux. Opening this page no longer auto-launches Termux.
When disabled, termux_exec only exposes command and timeout_ms, and PTY tools are not sent.
No active PTY server detected. It starts on demand when tty=true is used.
Attachments are disabled in command mode
Expose selected skills from %1$s to the model as a lightweight catalog.
Create a new skill scaffold or import a zip skill package into %1$s.
Enable Local Tools > Termux Command before using skills in chat.
Selections are saved, but the skills catalog will not be injected until the active model supports tool calls.
Use Create or Import zip, or manually add a subdirectory under %1$s with a SKILL.md containing name and description frontmatter.
Optional. Leave blank to use the default template.
Delete skill %1$s from %2$s? This cannot be undone.
This skill is still selected on the assistant, but it no longer exists in %1$s.
Play a notification sound when a tool call is waiting for approval
No quick messages available, create one in Extensions
No mode injections available, create one in Prompts
No lorebooks available, create one in Prompts
No skills available, install one in Extensions
Manage quick messages, prompt injections, lorebooks and skills
Create mode injections or lorebooks in Settings
Manage prompt injections, skills and more
Manage skill packages for AI to load on demand
Are you sure you want to delete "%s"? This action cannot be undone.
Tap the + button at the bottom right to add a quick message
Override the current Compose theme with user-defined color, shape, and typography tokens.
Write CSS-like theme tokens for color, shape, and type. Supported keys include primary, surfaceContainer, shapeLarge, fontScale, titleScale and more.
Format examples: primary: #RRGGBBAA; shapeLarge: 24dp; fontScale: 1.08; bodyScale: 98%; Also supports #RRGGBB and 0xAARRGGBB.
Recognized %1$d tokens. %2$d entries will be ignored.
The app crashed on the last launch. You can switch to a different assistant to resolve the issue.
Edit colors, radius, and type in one place without leaving the preview.
Derive matching on* foreground colors when a paired base color changes, unless that on* token is set explicitly.
These sliders expose the shape tokens that were previously only reachable in raw code.
Scale the entire type ramp or just one text group.
Use raw editing when you need exact token names, comments, or overrides not surfaced visually.
The visual editor now covers colors, radius, and typography in one screen.
Primary emphasis colors and their readable foregrounds.
Supporting accents for chips, cards, and alternate callouts.
Background layers and text colors used across the UI.
Error colors, outlines, scrims, and inverse surfaces.
Expressive variants used by larger controls.
Extra expressive radius for large panels.
Scales every text style before per-group adjustments.
Card titles and compact section headers.
Chips, helper text, and small labels.
Are you sure you want to delete file "%s"?
Global ST prompt preset applied to all assistants. Character cards and preset chat messages still stay bound to each assistant, while regex rules remain managed here.
No ST preset configured yet. Import a preset JSON or create the default preset.
Applies to all assistants by default. Good for shared ST preset regex, cleanup rules, and general formatting.
Only ST preset JSON files are supported here
Imported ST preset and appended %1$d global regex rules
This editor follows ST template fields, prompt order, and prompt definitions.
Long press the handle to reorder
Preset name plus Scenario, Personality, and World Info wrappers.
System prompt reuse, Continue behavior, and default injected texts.
Long press the left handle to reorder, toggle items on or off, and open details from settings.
No prompt items yet. Add a custom item or restore a default block first.
If disabled, the definition is kept but excluded from prompt order.
If the identifier is empty or duplicates an existing item, the original value is kept.
Built-in items use predefined slots. Choose from the list instead of typing raw identifiers.
Only affects list display, search, and export metadata. It does not change how this prompt is injected.
The app currently emits `normal` and `continue`. `quiet` is preserved mainly for ST compatibility and export. `impersonate` only matters when a compatibility path explicitly uses that mode.
Marker node. No prompt text is injected directly.
Tap to edit and fill in this prompt content.
This built-in item pulls its content from %1$s. Edit the source page instead of typing prompt text here.
example messages from the character card
Role is the field that actually controls the injected message role in the current app runtime.
The app does not currently expose a group-chat generation flow. These fields are kept mainly for ST compatibility and export, and they do not affect normal chats here.
Regular chat and Continue do not use these fields. They only apply when a compatibility path explicitly enters `impersonate` mode.

These values are preserved for ST compatibility, display, and export. They are not edited as normal runtime controls here. In the current app runtime, the fields that really affect behavior are `role`, `identifier`, and the injection settings.

Regex rules let you rewrite text before it is displayed, before it is sent to the model, or both.

Typical use cases:
- Clean up imported SillyTavern formatting.
- Hide boilerplate in the UI without changing saved messages.
- Normalize names or punctuation before the model sees them.
- Apply ST script placements such as user input, AI output, world info, slash commands, or reasoning.

Tips:
- Find Regex is a real regular expression.
- Replace String supports syntax like $1, ${name}, $<name>, and {{match}}.
- Trim captured strings removes extra text from captured groups before replacement is written back.
- RAW and ESCAPED substitution resolve macros like {{char}} and {{user}} inside the regex itself.
- If ST Placements is not empty, those placements override the generic scopes above.
If you select any ST placement here, it takes priority over the generic scopes above.
Use RAW or ESCAPED to resolve macros such as {{char}} and {{user}} inside the find regex before matching.
Tap to expand and configure this regex rule.
One line per value. These strings are removed from captured groups before replacement is written back.

This page separates the prompt systems so they do not pile into one screen.

SillyTavern Presets:
Use this when you want a shared ST prompt order, runtime prompts, sampling, and ST regex scripts.

Mode Injections:
Use this for manual or mode-based prompt blocks that behave like extra system instructions.

Lorebooks:
Use this for keyword-triggered world info. Each lorebook can be bound to specific assistants, and each entry can control scan depth, position, probability, recursion, grouping, and ST compatibility metadata.

Quick rule of thumb:
- Shared chat formatting: ST Preset
- Manual mode switch: Mode Injection
- Triggered knowledge: Lorebook

These fields mainly exist for SillyTavern compatibility. The practical runtime controls in this app cover grouping, recursion, delay, sticky, cooldown, budget, and generation triggers. Export-only fields are still preserved.
Allow triggered entries from this lorebook to activate more entries in later passes.

Use the lorebook editor to manage one book and its entry list.

Book-level fields:
- Name and description are for organization.
- Recursive Scanning lets triggered entries cause new entries to activate in later passes.
- Token Budget caps how much content from this book can be inserted.

Entry list:
- Each entry is an individual trigger block.
- Open an entry to edit keywords, content, injection position, probability, and ST advanced metadata.
- If an entry should always be active, enable Constant Active inside the entry editor instead of adding fake keywords.

This editor is for one lorebook entry.

Recommended workflow:
1. Add primary keywords that should activate the entry.
2. Turn on Use Regex only if plain keywords are not enough.
3. Choose where the content should be injected.
4. Fill the content with the exact fact or instruction you want the model to receive.
5. Only touch the Advanced Compatibility section when you are matching SillyTavern behavior or preparing an export.

Field guide:
- Primary keywords are the main trigger.
- Secondary keywords work with Selective mode for stricter matching.
- Trigger sources let the entry look at scenario, persona, creator notes, and other non-chat text.
- Generation types let you limit the entry to normal replies, continue, quiet, or impersonation flows.
- Group, sticky, cooldown, delay, recursion, and budget options are the ST-style controls for complex worldbook behavior.
These are the main trigger keywords. If Constant Active is on, the entry can activate without them.

Lorebooks are keyword-triggered world info.

How it works:
- A lorebook contains many entries.
- Each entry watches recent chat text and optional sources such as character description, scenario, persona, or creator notes.
- When an entry matches, its content is injected into the prompt at the position you choose.

What to edit here:
- Global settings control the default scan rules used across lorebooks in this app.
- Each lorebook can be enabled or disabled as a whole.
- Bind a lorebook to specific assistants if you do not want it shared everywhere.
- Open a lorebook to manage entries, recursion, token budget, probability, grouping, sticky effects, cooldown, and ST export metadata.

Practical advice:
- Keep entries short and specific.
- Use secondary keywords when one keyword is too broad.
- Use Constant Active sparingly so it does not crowd out the real conversation.
Use these with Selective mode when one keyword is too broad and you want a stricter trigger.
After a primary keyword matches, apply an extra rule to the secondary keywords.
Turn this off if you want to ignore the probability field and always allow activation.

Mode injections are manual prompt blocks. They are not keyword-triggered like lorebooks and they are not ST preset structure like SillyTavern presets.

Use them when you want a reusable mode, such as study mode, translation mode, or coding mode.

Priority controls order. Position controls where the block is inserted relative to the system prompt.
Manage world info entries, global scan settings, and assistant bindings.
Keep reusable mode-based injections separate from lorebooks and ST presets.
Manage multiple ST presets, switch the active one, and export back to ST JSON.
%1$d regex rules · %2$d sampling values · %3$d prompt items
Delete preset %1$s? This cannot be undone.

Controls whether speaker names are included in chat history. The Content mode is the one fully mirrored by the current in-app runtime; other options are preserved mainly for ST compatibility and export.

This page manages the shared SillyTavern preset library for the app.

How to use it:
- Import preset JSON from SillyTavern, or create a default preset here.
- The library drawer keeps many presets manageable. Pick one preset as the active shared preset.
- The preset editor controls prompt order, prompt definitions, runtime prompts, names behavior, sampling, and ST regex scripts.
- Assistant character cards and per-assistant prompt content still live with each assistant. This page is for the shared ST core.

Notes:
- Regex rules here are shared by default across assistants.
- Names Behavior is exposed for ST compatibility. The Content mode is the one fully mirrored by the current in-app runtime.
- Export from the library if you want to send the preset back to SillyTavern.
Search, switch, export, or delete presets without crowding the main editor.
No presets match the current search.
Manage shared ST presets, switch the active preset, and export.
Manage lorebooks, global switches, and assistant bindings.
Only character cards can be imported here
This page has been disabled for now.
This avatar and display name will be shown on the user side in chats.
Persona Description is used for `personaDescription`, `{{persona}}`, and related lorebook matching.
Structured editor for extensionSettings.%1$s. This covers the main merge labels and capture rules used by your target script.
Controls whether matched text is written into stored_data.
Range syntax matches the original script: `+1`, `-1`, `+1~+3`, `+1,+3~+5,-2`.
stored_data summary: no captured values yet
Detected %1$d accompanying regex rules. Preset regex rules will be imported into the current ST preset. Import them too?
Detected %1$d accompanying regex rules. Character card regex rules will be imported into the current assistant. Import them too?
Run a SillyTavern-style CHAT_COMPLETION_SETTINGS_READY script in Termux Node before OpenAI-compatible requests. This path is generation-only and does not recreate the original ST popup host.
Requires Termux with nodejs installed: `pkg install nodejs`.
Paste or import a SillyTavern compatibility script
No persona yet. Once created, it will drive the user avatar, display name, `personaDescription`, and `{{persona}}`.
The current persona does not have a description yet.
Start managing your user identity here. Each persona has its own avatar, display name, and Persona Description.
This avatar and display name are shown directly on the user side in chat.
Persona Description participates in `personaDescription`, `{{persona}}`, and related lorebook matching.
No assistants matched. Try clearing the search text or tag filters.
No assistants yet. Tap the add button in the top right, or start by cloning an existing configuration.
Tap to create and manage personas
Tap to switch and edit the current persona
Common parameters. Start here when adjusting randomness, candidate cutoff, and output length.
Control repetition suppression, candidate pruning, and topic drift. Usually only small adjustments are needed.
Stop conditions, seeds, and backend-specific extras. These only apply when the target backend supports them.
Controls reply randomness. Lower is steadier, higher is more divergent.
Filters the candidate pool by cumulative probability. Usually avoid maxing this out together with very high temperature.
Sample only from the highest-ranked K candidates. Lower is steadier, higher is more open.
Caps the maximum output tokens for one generation. Leave empty to use the backend default.
Encourages the model to introduce new content. Positive values shift topics more easily; negative values stick to existing topics.
Penalizes tokens based on how often they already appeared. Higher values reduce repeated wording and verbal tics.
Common on llama and OpenAI-compatible backends. 1.0 is neutral; higher values suppress repetition more strongly.
Sets a floor for low-probability token filtering. Higher values are more conservative and should only be changed when the backend clearly supports it.
Dynamically trims low-ranked tail tokens based on the candidate distribution. Most models do not need this changed.
One per line. Supported by OpenAI Chat Completions, Google, and Claude.
Supported by OpenAI-compatible and Google-compatible backends. Leave empty to use the backend default.
Google generationConfig.responseMimeType. Common values are text/plain and application/json.
OpenAI Responses API text verbosity. Use low, medium, or high.
OpenAI reasoning effort. Common values are low, medium, and high. Only applies to supported reasoning APIs.
When an ST preset is active, configured values here override assistant sampling settings.
The active preset overrides request params
Assistant params stay in effect until this preset is configured
Once at least one value is configured here, the active ST preset overrides assistant sampling. Blank fields stay blank and no longer fall back to assistant defaults.
Temperature, Top P, Top K, and Max Tokens shape randomness, candidate breadth, and output length. Start from this group first.
Penalties, Min P, Top A, Seed, and Reasoning effort are preserved when importing ST presets and only take effect on supported backends.
Responses API uses only verbosity. The sampler fields below are kept for compatibility and only affect Chat Completions-compatible endpoints.
Sent as OpenAI or OpenAI-compatible request fields.
Sent inside generationConfig for Gemini or Vertex-compatible backends.
Sent as Anthropic Messages API fields.
Only fields supported by the selected backend are used.
Controls how many recent rounds of executed tool calls and results remain in context. Older tool traces are removed while regular chat messages stay.
Not all models support depth adjustment. Refer to the model and provider documentation.
